reasoning: Peer sheet (left) shows classic small-scale Denzi pixel art sprites: tightly packed, ~16-32px tall, muted/earthy palette with cool accents, flat shading, 1px dark outlines, minimal highlight work, standard humanoid/creature proportions, charm is incidental not designed. Candidate (right) is a large chibi box-spirit with pronounced wings, a round white face, pastel blue/white/pink/gold palette—none of which has strong overlap with peer palette temperature. Six of eleven dims fail hard: (1) Outline weight—similar 1px but the candidate's per-pixel density at matched scale is lower, making outlines look thicker relatively; (2) Outline color—consistent dark, minor pass; (3) Shadow direction—no cast shadow in candidate, peers also lack them, marginal pass; (4) Shadow count—pass; (5) Highlight intensity—candidate has bright specular clusters on wings and face absent from entire peer sheet, fail; (6) Color temperature—candidate is cool-pastel dominant, peers are warm-neutral dominant, fail; (7) Detail density—at matched scale candidate is ~40% the detail density of peers, fail; (8) Proportion—candidate is aggressively chibi (head ≥ 50% body height), peers are standard RPG proportions, fail; (9) Rendering style—candidate uses gradient-like dithered shading for a 'painterly' feel; peers use pure flat pixel art, fail; (10) Palette overlap—roughly 2 shared hues out of ~8 dominant hues, fail; (11) Charm level—candidate is deliberately kawaii/cute-designed, peers are incidentally charming via classic pixel craft, fail. 6 hard fails out of 11 dims. Even granting item-type leniency, the stylistic gulf is too wide for a same-family pass.
